mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS stays near zero while CPIAUCSL is firm, rate markets infer continued accommodation and stronger inflation compensation. That combination lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days as investors price a slower path to normalization.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are negative in most low-rate/high-CPI observations, or if the sign flips after conditioning on FEDFUNDS near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above 6.5% and PAYEMS is still soft, markets price weaker demand and less inflation pressure. That mix pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days through lower expected growth and easier policy expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes show a positive 20-day yield change more than half the time, or if higher UNRATE does not coincide with lower yields after a one-month lag.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in RSAFS and HOUST signals stronger private demand and firmer nominal activity. That should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days as investors revise growth and inflation expectations upward.
failure_condition: Reject if joint strength in retail sales and housing starts does not outperform either series alone, or if the 20-day yield response remains negative after both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while GDPC1 is already soft, the market reads a broad output gap and diminished inflation capacity. That should push the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained industrial production declines fail to coincide with falling yields over 20 days, or if the effect vanishes once GDP is held fixed.

mechanism_narrative: A Fed rate increase above 1.0% after a prior low-rate period tightens financial conditions and lifts term premia. That should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices the policy path.
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike triggers are followed by flat or lower 5-day yield moves in most cases, or if the sign is not positive within one week of the crossing.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, labor slack is increasing faster than wage pressure can build. That pattern should lower the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days through weaker growth expectations and reduced inflation risk.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-slab deterioration does not produce lower yields in triggered windows, or if PAYEMS declines alone explain the sign without UNRATE rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS after a weak GDP print indicates demand is re-accelerating despite prior slowdown. That should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations broaden beyond the latest quarterly GDP lag.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month retail sales rises are not followed by higher 60-day yields, or if the response is negative when GDP is below its prior-quarter level.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and the market demands more inflation compensation. This combination should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days, but only in the low-rate inflation regime.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up/low-rate episodes do not produce positive 20-day yield changes, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is above its recent average.
